assertions.expectation: Explains that exact upgrade dates are difficult to predict but several tools help narrow the window
assertions.reason: The response focuses heavily on control mechanisms but doesn't clearly explain that exact upgrade dates are inherently difficult to predict due to GKE's rollout model

assertions.expectation: Mentions the GKE release schedule as a 'best case' timeline — upgrades won't happen before listed dates
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention the GKE release schedule or explain it as a 'best case' timeline for when upgrades won't happen before certain dates

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking the cluster's auto-upgrade status for the current target version and any pauses
assertions.reason: While the response shows how to disable auto-upgrades, it doesn't recommend checking current auto-upgrade status or target versions as a visibility tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions that new releases roll out progressively across regions over 4-5 business days, and the version promotion path includes channel promotion (Rapid → Regular → Stable)
assertions.reason: The response mentions release channels but doesn't explain the progressive regional rollout timeline or the channel promotion pathway

assertions.expectation: Recommends configuring maintenance windows so upgrades only happen during known time slots. May note that for ultimate predictability, customers can initiate upgrades themselves instead of waiting for auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: The response clearly shows how to configure maintenance windows and demonstrates manual upgrade initiation for maximum control

assertions.expectation: Mentions scheduled upgrade notifications (72h advance, opt-in via Cloud Logging) or the get-upgrade-info API for checking target versions programmatically
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention the 72-hour advance notifications via Cloud Logging or the get-upgrade-info API specifically for checking target versions

assertions.expectation: Notes that GKE upgrade visibility shows the cluster's target version and EoL timeline
assertions.reason: While the response shows some API calls for cluster information, it doesn't specifically mention target version visibility or EoL timeline features

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing (advanced feature, limited adoption) as an option for multi-cluster predictability — not a primary recommendation
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention rollout sequencing as an advanced GKE feature, though it does show a custom staged upgrade approach
